nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Smith II was born in 1430, in Exeter, Devon, England, his father, Sir John William Smythe, was 74 and his mother, Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, was 31. He married Amyas E. Scott in 1458, in Exeter, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1490, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Exeter, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Smith II est né en 1430, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir John William Smythe, avait 74 ans et sa mère, Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Amyas E. Scott en 1458, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1490, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Smith II 1430 in Exeter, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John William Smythe 74 und seine Mutter Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham 31. Er hat 1458 in Exeter, Devon, England Amyas E. Scott geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Exeter, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Smith II è nato il nel 1430 nel Exeter, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir John William Smythe, aveva 74 anni e il suo madre, Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Amyas E. Scott nel 1458, nel Exeter, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1490, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Exeter, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Smith IIが1430年にイギリスデボンエクセターで生まれた時，父Sir John William Smytheは74歳，母Lady Millicent Elizabeth Lainghamは31歳であった。1458年にイギリスデボンエクセターでAmyas E. Scottと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1490年に故郷で60歳で死去し，イギリスデボンエクセターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Smith II(이)가 1430년 영국 데폰 세터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John William Smythe(은)는 74세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1458년 영국 데폰 세터에서 Amyas E. Scott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 세터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Smith II nasceu em 1430, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John William Smythe, tinha 74 anos e sua mãe, Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Amyas E. Scott em 1458, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1490, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Smith II родился в 1430 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John William Smythe было 74 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Amyas E. Scott в 1458 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1490 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности эксетир, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Smith II nació en 1430, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John William Smythe, tenía 74 años y su mamá, Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Amyas E. Scott en 1458, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1490, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.
